executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations publicly listed as victims on June 27, 2026. High-profile breaches include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and Kids & Company childcare. The Qilin ransomware group alone claimed 21 Canadian victims in a single day, while Clop claimed 13 and Play claimed 11. Critical sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector municipalities, financial services, and manufacturing—this represents a coordinated mass exploitation event targeting Canadian infrastructure.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Canada Goose, Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, and multiple manufacturing/construction firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion ransomware with public leak site

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including law firms (SMT Lawyers), technology companies (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions), energy sector (East Plats), hospitality (Whiski Jack Resorts), and manufacturing
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - known for mass exploitation campaigns

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Makivik, Security ONE Alarm Systems, and multiple manufacturing firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations across transportation (Briway Carriers), agriculture (Matt and Steve, RIP Quebec), construction (Groupe Piche, Kenalex, Silverline Group), education (Teccart), and manufacturing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe coordinated ransomware campaign with 103 organizations publicly listed as victims on June 27, 2026. Critical infrastructure sectors heavily impacted include public health (Peterborough Public Health), municipal government (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart, Bais Yaakov), healthcare (multiple medical facilities), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, Trucash, Morison Insurance). No official CCCS advisories were identified in this dataset.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer services/retail), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), Kids & Company (childcare services)
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked - Everest ransomware), Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash, multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant

report_2w.executive_summary: Fortinet devices are under active threat following the FortiBleed credential leak affecting thousands of organizations—Canadian cybersecurity authority CCCS issued a formal alert. Cisco Unified CM (CVE-2026-20230) is being actively exploited in the wild to drop webshells after a public proof-of-concept was released. LastPass suffered another supply chain breach via compromised vendor Klue, exposing customer support data stored in Salesforce. Three critical Ubiquiti UniFi OS vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-34908, 34909, 34910) allowing remote code execution without authentication are now on CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list. Iran-linked MuddyWater APT is disguising espionage operations as ransomware attacks.

items.description: Cisco Unified Communications Manager file-write vulnerability allows webshell deployment leading to root access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: MuddyWater (APT group, Iran-linked)
items.targets: Various organizations globally; masquerading operations as ransomware attacks
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - fake ransomware), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - cyber espionage disguised as ransomware operations

items.name: KongTuke (Woodgnat) - Ransomware Access Broker
items.targets: Financial sector organizations
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1505.003 (Web Shell), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - deploying Mistic/MTLBackdoor for ransomware access

items.name: Icarus
items.targets: LastPass via Klue supply chain attack; accessed Salesforce customer support data
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship), T1550.001 (Application Access Token - OAuth token theft), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories)

items.name: StrikeShark Campaign Operators
items.targets: Enterprise infrastructure including Cisco, F5, Microsoft Exchange, Fortinet devices
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1059.001 (PowerShell), T1071.001 (Web Protocols - Cobalt Strike C2), exploiting CVE-2016-4437, CVE-2021-26855, CVE-2022-41082, CVE-2023-20198, CVE-2024-21762, CVE-2025-55182 via SharkLoader

items.name: Akira Ransomware Group
items.targets: Jit Ex, Miami Machine
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Qilin Ransomware Group

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Scattered Spider

items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1566 (Phishing), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol)

items.name: North Korean APT (Microsoft attribution)

items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1059.007 (JavaScript), targeting npm ecosystem

items.ttps: T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1204.002 (Malicious File), T1036.005 (Match Legitimate Name or Location - fake updates), disrupted by Europol operation

items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada's cybersecurity authority CCCS issued alert AL26-014 warning of the FortiBleed credential leak affecting thousands of Fortinet devices. Canadian utility London Hydro reported a data breach following system vulnerability exploitation. Cash Canada was hit by Qilin ransomware with data leaked on dark web. Canadian organizations should prioritize patching Fortinet, Cisco, and Ubiquiti devices immediately.
canada_landscape.financial: Cash Canada - victim of Qilin ransomware attack with data exfiltrated
